---
format: 
  html:
    theme: RLadies-styles.scss
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 3
    smooth-scroll: true
---

::: {.callout-tip collapse=true}
## A git config file is a place to define your Git settings and preferences

This includes things like setting your `user.name` and `user.email`, which are used to identify the author of commits (you may have done this recently as part of the initial setup prior to this workshop). It's also a place for us to permenently set the name of our default branch for all future git repositories. You can view the contents of your entire git config file by running the following command in your Terminal:

```{r filename="Terminal"}
#| eval: false    
git config --list
```
:::
